_comment: Skill Graph rebuild Phase 3 (2026-07-23) — canonical A1 curriculum. Sourced from the standard CEFR progression (the same public grammar/function/vocabulary framework EAQUALS/Cambridge English Profile use), sequenced the way mainstream coursebooks (Empower/Headway/English File) order A1 topics — informed by their structure, not copied text. Consumed by POST /api/admin/skill-graph/nodes/import (idempotent upsert by key). Every prerequisiteKey below resolves within this same file (A1 has no earlier level).

nodes.title: Subject pronouns (I, you, he, she, it, we, they)
nodes.description: Identifying the person doing an action before learning any verb forms.
nodes.descriptionForAi: Root grammar node — nearly everything else depends on this.

nodes.title: Verb 'to be': am/is/are (statements)
nodes.description: Using am/is/are to state names, ages, nationalities, and locations.

nodes.title: Verb 'to be': negatives and questions
nodes.description: Forming negative statements and yes/no or wh- questions with am/is/are.

nodes.title: Possessive adjectives (my, your, his, her, our, their)
nodes.description: Showing ownership before talking about family and belongings.

nodes.title: Articles: a, an, the
nodes.description: Choosing the correct article before a singular countable noun.

nodes.title: Plural nouns (regular -s and common irregulars)
nodes.description: Forming plurals to describe more than one person or thing.

nodes.title: Demonstratives: this, that, these, those
nodes.description: Pointing out objects by physical distance (near vs far).

nodes.title: There is / There are
nodes.description: Describing what exists in a place, room, or town.

nodes.title: Present simple: affirmative for daily routines
nodes.description: Talking about habits, routines, and general facts.
nodes.descriptionForAi: Second most load-bearing root node after subject pronouns/to be.

nodes.title: Present simple: negatives and questions with do/does
nodes.description: Using don't/doesn't and do/does to negate and ask about routines.

nodes.title: Adverbs of frequency (always, usually, sometimes, never)
nodes.description: Adding frequency detail to present simple routine sentences.

nodes.title: Imperatives for instructions and directions
nodes.description: Giving simple commands and instructions (Stop! Turn left. Open the door.).

nodes.title: Can/can't for ability and permission
nodes.description: Expressing physical ability and simple permission.

nodes.title: Present continuous for actions happening now
nodes.description: Using am/is/are + -ing to describe what is happening right now.

nodes.title: Prepositions of place (in, on, at, next to, near)
nodes.description: Locating people and objects relative to other things.

nodes.title: Prepositions of time (in, on, at)
nodes.description: Talking about when things happen (at 9am, on Monday, in July).

nodes.title: Object pronouns (me, him, her, us, them)
nodes.description: Replacing a noun object with a pronoun in a simple sentence.

nodes.title: Past simple of 'to be' (was/were)
nodes.description: Talking about where someone was and how something used to be.

nodes.title: Have got for possession
nodes.description: Describing what someone owns or has using have/has got.

nodes.title: Question words (who, what, where, when, how)
nodes.description: Building information questions with wh- words plus to be/do.
nodes.descriptionForAi: Load-bearing for most Speaking/Writing question-asking nodes.

nodes.title: Numbers 1-100
nodes.description: Cardinal numbers for counting, prices, and ages.

nodes.title: The alphabet and spelling aloud
nodes.description: Naming letters and spelling names/words out loud.

nodes.title: Days of the week, months, and dates
nodes.description: Naming days, months, and saying/writing simple dates.

nodes.title: Telling the time
nodes.description: Saying and understanding clock times.

nodes.title: Family members
nodes.description: Words for immediate and extended family relationships.

nodes.title: Countries and nationalities
nodes.description: Naming countries and the matching nationality adjective.

nodes.title: Jobs and occupations
nodes.description: Common job titles for talking about work.

nodes.title: Colors
nodes.description: Basic color words used as adjectives.

nodes.title: Clothes
nodes.description: Everyday clothing items and simple descriptions of what people wear.

nodes.title: Food and drink
nodes.description: Common food/drink items for meals, menus, and shopping.

nodes.title: Classroom and everyday objects
nodes.description: Common object nouns used constantly in early lessons.

nodes.title: House, rooms, and furniture
nodes.description: Vocabulary for rooms in a house and common furniture.

nodes.title: Parts of the body
nodes.description: Basic body-part nouns.

nodes.title: Adjectives describing people and things
nodes.description: Common descriptive adjectives (tall, happy, big, old, etc.).

nodes.title: Daily routine verbs
nodes.description: Verbs for everyday actions (wake up, eat, work, sleep).

nodes.title: Hobbies and free time activities
nodes.description: Words for common leisure activities.

nodes.title: Weather
nodes.description: Basic weather words and simple weather descriptions.

nodes.title: Places in town
nodes.description: Common locations (bank, station, supermarket, hospital) for giving/getting directions.

nodes.title: Transport and travel words
nodes.description: Vocabulary for common ways of travelling (bus, train, car, plane).

nodes.title: Shopping and money
nodes.description: Prices, currency words, and simple shopping vocabulary.

nodes.title: Matching words to pictures
nodes.description: Connecting single words/short phrases to matching images.

nodes.title: Recognizing common signs and notices
nodes.description: Reading short public signs (Exit, No smoking, Open/Closed).

nodes.title: Reading a simple personal profile
nodes.description: Understanding a short text giving someone's name, age, job, and family.

nodes.title: Reading short messages and texts
nodes.description: Understanding short everyday messages (texts, notes) about routines and plans.

nodes.title: Reading a simple menu or price list
nodes.description: Finding items and prices in a menu or shop price list.

nodes.title: Reading a short email or postcard
nodes.description: Understanding a short informal email/postcard about a holiday or news.

nodes.title: Reading a simple timetable or schedule
nodes.description: Finding times and destinations in a bus/train timetable.

nodes.title: Identifying the main topic of a short text
nodes.description: Deciding what a short simple text is mainly about.

nodes.title: True/false comprehension of simple statements
nodes.description: Deciding whether simple statements about a text are true or false.

nodes.title: Following simple written instructions
nodes.description: Reading and following a short numbered set of instructions.

nodes.title: Recognizing spoken numbers
nodes.description: Understanding numbers said aloud (prices, phone numbers, ages).

nodes.title: Understanding greetings and introductions
nodes.description: Recognizing spoken greetings and how people introduce themselves.

nodes.title: Listening for key words
nodes.description: Picking out familiar key words in slow, clear speech.

nodes.title: Understanding simple yes/no questions
nodes.description: Recognizing spoken yes/no questions with to be.

nodes.title: Understanding short everyday dialogues
nodes.description: Following a short two-person conversation about a familiar topic.

nodes.title: Following simple spoken directions
nodes.description: Understanding spoken directions to a place in town.

nodes.title: Identifying spoken times and dates
nodes.description: Understanding times and dates said aloud.

nodes.title: Understanding simple public announcements
nodes.description: Following short, slow public announcements (station, airport).

nodes.title: Recognizing letters spelled aloud
nodes.description: Understanding a name or word being spelled out loud.

nodes.title: Understanding basic spoken descriptions
nodes.description: Following a short spoken description of a person or object.

nodes.title: Using capital letters and full stops
nodes.description: Basic sentence punctuation and capitalization conventions.

nodes.title: Writing your name and basic personal information
nodes.description: Writing name, age, nationality, and job in short sentences.

nodes.title: Writing simple affirmative and negative statements
nodes.description: Constructing simple present-simple sentences correctly.

nodes.title: Writing about your family
nodes.description: Short sentences describing family members and relationships.

nodes.title: Writing short descriptive sentences
nodes.description: Describing a person, place, or object with simple adjectives.

nodes.title: Filling in a simple form
nodes.description: Completing a basic form with personal details.

nodes.title: Writing a short postcard or note
nodes.description: A short informal message describing where you are and what you're doing.

nodes.title: Writing simple lists
nodes.description: Making a shopping list or to-do list with single words/short phrases.

nodes.title: Describing your daily routine in writing
nodes.description: A short paragraph describing a typical day using present simple.

nodes.title: Writing short answers to questions
nodes.description: Answering simple wh- and yes/no questions in full short sentences.

nodes.title: Greetings and farewells
nodes.description: Basic greeting/leave-taking exchanges (Hello, how are you? Goodbye, see you.).

nodes.title: Introducing yourself
nodes.description: Giving your name, nationality, job, and basic personal info aloud.

nodes.title: Asking for someone's personal information
nodes.description: Asking simple questions to learn someone's name, age, or job.

nodes.title: Describing your daily routine aloud
nodes.description: Talking through a typical day using present simple and routine vocabulary.

nodes.title: Talking about your family
nodes.description: Describing family members and relationships in conversation.

nodes.title: Ordering food and drink
nodes.description: A simple roleplay ordering at a cafe or restaurant.

nodes.title: Asking for and giving simple directions
nodes.description: A roleplay asking how to get somewhere and giving basic directions.

nodes.title: Simple shopping transactions
nodes.description: A roleplay buying something and handling prices/money.

nodes.title: Talking about likes and dislikes
nodes.description: Expressing simple preferences (I like.../I don't like...).

nodes.title: Describing people and things aloud
nodes.description: Using basic adjectives to describe appearance and objects in speech.

nodes.title: Telling the time aloud
nodes.description: Saying clock times correctly in conversation.

nodes.title: Talking about the weather
nodes.description: Simple small-talk exchanges about today's weather.

nodes.title: Sounds of the alphabet
nodes.description: Producing the individual sounds/names of English letters.
nodes.descriptionForAi: Root of the A1 pronunciation progression: sounds -> vowels -> th -> word stress -> sentence-level stress/intonation -> linking.

nodes.title: Short and long vowel sounds
nodes.description: Distinguishing short/long vowel sounds in common words.

nodes.title: The 'th' sounds
nodes.description: Producing voiced and unvoiced 'th' sounds correctly.

nodes.title: Word stress in common words
nodes.description: Stressing the correct syllable in everyday high-frequency words.

nodes.title: Sentence stress in questions
nodes.description: Placing stress naturally when asking simple questions.

nodes.title: Rising intonation in yes/no questions
nodes.description: Using rising intonation naturally when asking yes/no questions.

nodes.title: Linking sounds between words
nodes.description: Connecting word-final and word-initial sounds naturally in short phrases.

nodes.title: Using fixed greeting phrases automatically
nodes.description: Producing common greetings without hesitation or translation.

nodes.title: Giving a short, pause-free self-introduction
nodes.description: Delivering a memorized self-introduction smoothly, without long pauses.

nodes.title: Responding quickly to simple questions
nodes.description: Answering very familiar yes/no or personal questions with minimal delay.

nodes.title: Using 'and'/'but' to link simple ideas
nodes.description: Joining two short ideas smoothly instead of pausing between them.

nodes.title: Repeating common phrases naturally
nodes.description: Reproducing frequent chunks (nice to meet you, see you later) at natural speed.

nodes.title: Willingness to speak in simple exchanges
nodes.description: Attempting a short spoken exchange even with limited language.

nodes.title: Asking for repetition or clarification
nodes.description: Using simple phrases (Sorry? Can you repeat that?) when something isn't understood.

nodes.title: Self-rating basic progress
nodes.description: Reflecting simply on what you can/can't yet say in English.

nodes.title: Using gestures to support communication
nodes.description: Supplementing limited language with gesture/mime to get a message across.

nodes.title: Attempting new words without fear of mistakes
nodes.description: Trying unfamiliar vocabulary aloud rather than avoiding it.
